agent.runner_summary: The simulator adapter builds the SkyServe airline support Agent and runs each fixed turn's content string through Runner.run, returning the agent's final text output.

components.when_to_use: Scenarios about looking up an existing demo booking (route, departure, seat, status) by confirmation code, including invalid or unknown codes.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"confirmation_code": "SKY123"}) with a demo code from BOOKINGS (SKY123, BAY777) or an invalid code to test the not-found path. Do not use positional args.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned booking summary string, or the not-found message when the confirmation code is unknown.

components.when_to_use: Scenarios about baggage allowance questions for a given ticket type (basic economy, standard, premium/business/first).
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"ticket_type": "standard"}) with one of basic/standard/business ticket type strings. The argument is optional and defaults to "standard".
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned baggage policy sentence for the requested ticket tier.

components.when_to_use: Scenarios about changing the seat on an existing demo booking after confirmation-code verification.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"confirmation_code": "SKY123", "requested_seat": "14C"}). This tool mutates the shared in-memory BOOKINGS dataset, so mock it in agent-level scenarios; do not use positional args.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned seat-confirmation message, or the invalid-confirmation-code message for unknown codes.

log_notes: Terminal session logs under logs/*.jsonl contain one JSON object per line: a leading {"type": "session", ...} record followed by {"type": "message", "role": "user"|"assistant", "content": ...} records.
When turning a log into a learning environment, the adapter-facing turn value is the plain user message content string, not the full JSONL event object.
learning_env_guidance: The agent selects its model from environment keys: OPENAI_API_KEY uses the default OpenAI Agents SDK model, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (checked only when OPENAI_API_KEY is unset) uses litellm/anthropic/claude-sonnet-5. Prefer whichever key is already configured in the runtime environment; do not require both.
